rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings only.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers or locally declared helpers.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Use only AGREE-supported scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations and expressions.
Ensure type consistency between assumed types and actual expression types in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared within the same annex where they are referenced.
Package-qualified identifiers in AGREE must match configured `with` clauses in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers or types in AGREE contracts; remove invalid clauses instead of adding architecture.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings only.
Do not mix package-qualified names in AGREE unless explicitly configured in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
